revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 加佐登神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 三重県鈴鹿市加佐登町2010 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|06|N|136|31|25|E|scale:5000}} |祭神 = 日本武尊 |社格 = |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月8日 |神事 = 4月8日（大祭） |}}
'''加佐登神社'''（かさどじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊（ヤマトタケル）]]の能褒野（のぼの）陵墓と伝えられる[[白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)|白鳥塚古墳]]の横に鎮座し、尊が死の間際まで持っていたといわれる笠と杖をご神体として祀る。紫[[ツツジ]]の名所として知られる。
==概要== 日本武尊を主祭神とし、古来、御笠殿社（みかさどのしゃ）と呼ばれていた。 江戸時代後期の国学者・[[平田篤胤]]は「御笠殿社由来記」（[[1829年]]）で、腫物などの病に霊験あると記している。 [[明治6年]]に現社名へ改め、[[明治41年]]に近隣の神社16社を合祀、現在の姿となった。
境内の椎山川中世墓から出土した陶器などを展示する高宮資料館がある。
==祭事・年中行事== 大祭：日本武尊の命日といわれる4月8日</br>例祭：10月8日
==所在地・交通== 三重県鈴鹿市加佐登町2010 *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩30分
==周辺情報== 一帯は能褒野と呼ばれ、日本武尊を葬ったと伝えられる白鳥塚古墳を始め、着物を納めたと伝えられる綺宮碕奉装塚・奉冠塚など、日本武尊の旧跡が多い。
{{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かさとしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:鈴鹿市]]

{{神社 |名称 = 加佐登神社 |画像 = [[File:KasadoJinjya.JPG|240px]] |所在地 = 三重県鈴鹿市加佐登町2010 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 日本武尊 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月8日 |神事 = 4月8日（大祭） |}}
'''加佐登神社'''（かさどじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の能褒野（のぼの）陵墓と伝えられた[[白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)|白鳥塚古墳]]の横に鎮座し、尊が死の間際まで持っていたといわれる笠と杖をご神体として祀る。</br> [[延喜式神名帳]]にある、[[伊勢国]][[鈴鹿郡]]の'''[[倭文神社]]'''（しとりじんじゃ）を合祀したと伝えられる。 紫[[ツツジ]]の名所としても知られている。
==概要== 日本武尊を主祭神とし、古来、御笠殿社（みかさどのしゃ）と呼ばれていた。 [[江戸時代]]後期の国学者・[[平田篤胤]]は「御笠殿社由来記」（[[1829年]]）で、腫物などの病に霊験あると記している。 境内には倭文神社や[[慈悲山廃寺]]があったと伝えられる。</br> [[明治6年]]（[[1873年]]）に「笠殿」（かさどの）から現社名へ改め、[[明治41年]]（[[1908年]]）に近隣の神社16社を合祀し、現在の姿となった。また同年、[[村社]]に列格した。
境内の椎山川中世墓から出土した陶器などを展示する高宮資料館がある。
==祭事・年中行事== 大祭：日本武尊の命日といわれる4月8日</br>例祭：10月8日
==所在地・交通== 三重県鈴鹿市加佐登町2010 *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩30分
==周辺情報== 一帯は能褒野と呼ばれ、日本武尊を葬ったと伝えられる白鳥塚古墳を始め、着物を納めたと伝えられる綺宮碕奉装塚・奉冠塚など、日本武尊の旧跡が多い。 *[[観音寺 (鈴鹿市高塚町)|観音寺]] *鈴鹿フラワーパーク

{{神社 |名称 = 加佐登神社 |画像 = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|260px]] |所在地 = 三重県鈴鹿市加佐登町2010 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=加佐登神社}} |祭神 = 日本武尊 |社格 = 村社 |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = 10月8日 |神事 = 4月8日（大祭） |}} '''加佐登神社'''（かさどじんじゃ）は[[三重県]][[鈴鹿市]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の能褒野（のぼの）陵墓と伝えられた[[白鳥塚古墳 (鈴鹿市)|白鳥塚古墳]]の横に鎮座し、尊が死の間際まで持っていたといわれる笠と杖をご神体として祀る。
[[延喜式神名帳]]にある、[[伊勢国]][[鈴鹿郡]]の'''[[倭文神社]]'''（しとりじんじゃ）を合祀したと伝えられる。 紫[[ツツジ]]の名所としても知られている。 ==概要== 日本武尊を主祭神とし、古来、御笠殿社（みかさどのしゃ）と呼ばれていた。 [[江戸時代]]後期の国学者・[[平田篤胤]]は「御笠殿社由来記」（[[1829年]]）で、腫物などの病に霊験あると記している。 境内には倭文神社や[[慈悲山廃寺]]があったと伝えられる。
[[明治6年]]（[[1873年]]）に「笠殿」（かさどの）から現社名へ改め、[[明治41年]]（[[1908年]]）に近隣の神社16社を合祀し、現在の姿となった。また同年、[[村社]]に列格した。 境内の椎山川中世墓から出土した陶器などを展示する高宮資料館がある。 ==祭事== * 月次祭 （毎月8日） * 歳旦祭 （1月1日） * 祈年祭 （2月21日） * 春季大祭 （4月8日） - 通称「八日さん」。日本武尊の薨じた日とされる。 * 天王祭 （7月31日） - 茅の輪くぐりが行われる。 * 例祭 （10月8日） * 新嘗祭 （11月30日） == 境内 == Image:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|高宮資料館 Image:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|参道 Image:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|木製鳥居 Image:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|稲荷神社 Image:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|日本武尊像 ==所在地・交通== 三重県鈴鹿市加佐登町2010 *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[加佐登駅]]下車徒歩20分 *JR加佐登駅・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[平田町駅]]から[[C-BUS (コミュニティバス)|C-BUS]]椿・平田線に乗り換えて「加佐登神社」バス停下車徒歩5分 ==周辺情報== {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}} 一帯は能褒野と呼ばれ、日本武尊を葬ったと伝えられる白鳥塚古墳を始め、着物を納めたと伝えられる綺宮碕奉装塚・奉冠塚など、日本武尊の旧跡が多い。 *[[観音寺 (鈴鹿市高塚町)|観音寺]] *鈴鹿フラワーパーク == 外部リンク == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ 加佐登神社] - 公式サイト {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かさとしんしや}} [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:三重県の旧村社]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の歴史]] [[Category:鈴鹿市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Kasado Shrine |Image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |Location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |Deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Annual Festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * **Monthly Festival**: Held on the 8th of every month. * **New Year Festival**: January 1. * **Prayer Festival**: February 21. * **Spring Grand Festival**: April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * **Tenno Festival**: July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * **Annual Festival**: October 8. * **Harvest Festival**: November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== Location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Shrine |Name = Kasado Shrine |Image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |Location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |Deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Annual Festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''Annual Festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== Location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''Annual Festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

{{Shrine |Name = Kasado Shrine |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Annual Festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''Annual Festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Shrine |Name = Kasado Shrine |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |Coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other Names = |Annual Festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''Annual Festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''Annual Festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Shrine |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratori Mound|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Approach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|Aqqroach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]
