revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.

In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill| Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=draft}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja|12=draft}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=draft}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park

The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft|12=draft}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja|12=draft|12=draft}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=draft|12=draft}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft|12=draft}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja|12=draft|12=draft}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=draft|12=draft}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District|ja|鈴鹿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise Province|ja|伊勢国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park

Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirata Atsutane|qq=}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[Kasado Station]] on the [[Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398475]] Wikidata name: Kasado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG|250px]] |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{coord|34|54|00|N|136|31|38|E|region:JP-24_type:landmark_scale:5000|display=inline,title|name=Kasado Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suzuka|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398475]] Wikidata name: Kasado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11398475]] Wikidata name: Kasado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.9|N|136.527222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) |location = {{ill|Suzuka|qid=Q207311}} }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suzuka|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suzuka|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suzuka|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suzuka|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kasado-jinja]][[ja:加佐登神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kasado-jinja]][[ja:加佐登神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kasado-jinja]][[ja:加佐登神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kasado-jinja]][[ja:加佐登神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398475]] Wikidata name: Kasado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398475]] Wikidata name: Kasado-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:03, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]] |coordinates = {{Coord|34.9|N|136.527222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suzuka|Suzuka]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for [[:en:Suzuka District, Mie|Suzuka District]], [[:en:Ise Province|Ise Province]]. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]], a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] on the [[:en:Kansai Main Line|Kansai Main Line]] operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From [[:en:Kasado Station|Kasado Station]] or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suzuka|en|Suzuka|WD=Q406474|lt=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|WD=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line}} operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.

The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
