{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.9|N|136.527222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311}} }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suzuka|en|Suzuka|WD=Q406474|lt=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|WD=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line}} operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|WD=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line}} operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station}} or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.9|N|136.527222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}} }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suzuka|en|Suzuka|WD=Q406474|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|WD=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District|1=Suzuka District, Mie}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Kasado Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:加佐登神社 - 拝殿2.JPG |location = [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|鈴鹿市加佐登町2010]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.9|N|136.527222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} |Rank = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = |Other names = |festival = October 8 |Rituals = April 8 (Grand Festival) |location = {{ill|Suzuka|WD=Q207311|1=Suzuka, Mie}} }}
'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suzuka|en|Suzuka|WD=Q406474|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|WD=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District|1=Suzuka District, Mie}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|WD=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station|1=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|WD=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|WD=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station|1=Kasado Station}} or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]

'''Kasado Shrine''' (加佐登神社, Kasado Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suzuka|en|Suzuka|qid=Q406474|lt=Suzuka|1=Suzuka}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan.
== Overview == The shrine is located beside the {{ill|Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|lt=Shiratori Kofun|ja|白鳥塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiratorizuka Kofun (Suzuka)|qq=}}, traditionally believed to be the mausoleum of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}. The shrine enshrines Yamato Takeru’s cape and staff, said to have been with him in his final moments.
The shrine also claims historical ties to {{ill|Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|lt=Shitori Shrine|ja|倭文神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitori Shrine|qq=}}, listed in the [[Engishiki|Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] for {{ill|Suzuka District, Mie|en|Suzuka District, Mie|qid=Q278828|lt=Suzuka District|1=Suzuka District, Mie}}, {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|qid=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}}. It is also famous as a spot for purple azaleas.
Historically, the shrine was called "Mikasadono Shrine" (御笠殿社), referring to its association with Yamato Takeru. {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|en|Hirata Atsutane|qid=Q2572749|lt=Hirata Atsutane|1=Hirata Atsutane}}, a late-Edo period scholar, described its efficacy in curing swellings and other illnesses in his work "御笠殿社由来記" (1829).
In [[Meiji 6 (1873)|1873]], the shrine was renamed from "Kasadono" to its current name. In 1908, 16 nearby shrines were merged into Kasado Shrine, which was then Ranked as a {{ill|village shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/village shrine|qq=}}.
The Takamiya Museum on the shrine grounds displays artifacts, including ceramics, excavated from medieval burial sites along the Shiyama River.
== Festivals and Rituals == * '''Monthly Festival''': Held on the 8th of every month. * '''New Year Festival''': January 1. * '''Prayer Festival''': February 21. * '''Spring Grand Festival''': April 8 ("Yōka-san"), commemorating Yamato Takeru's death. * '''Tenno Festival''': July 31, featuring a [[chinowa kuguri]] (passing through a ring of grass). * '''festival''': October 8. * '''Harvest Festival''': November 30.
== Grounds == <gallery widths="150px" heights="150px"> File:加佐登神社 - 高宮資料館.JPG|Takamiya Museum File:加佐登神社 - 参道2.JPG|A11roach File:加佐登神社 - 木製鳥居.JPG|Wooden torii gate File:加佐登神社 - 稲荷神社 - 赤鳥居.JPG|Inari Shrine File:加佐登神社 - 日本武尊像2.jpg|Statue of Yamato Takeru File:加佐登神社 - 御百度石.JPG|Omohyakudo Stone </gallery>
== location == * [[Kasado Town, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture|Kasado Town 2010, Suzuka City, Mie Prefecture]]
== Access == * By train: 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|qid=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station|1=Kasado Station}} on the {{ill|Kansai Main Line|en|Kansai Main Line|qid=Q1199703|lt=Kansai Main Line|1=Kansai Main Line}} operated by [[:en:JR Central|JR Central]]. * By bus: From {{ill|Kasado Station|en|Kasado Station|qid=Q5358774|lt=Kasado Station|1=Kasado Station}} or [[:en:Hiratacho Station|Hiratacho Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Suzuka Line|Kintetsu Suzuka Line]], take the C-BUS Tsubaki-Hirata Line to "Kasado Shrine" stop, then walk 5 minutes.
== Nearby Points of Interest == {{Commonscat|Kasado-jinja}}
The surrounding area, known as {{ill|Nobono|ja|能褒野|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nobono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is rich in sites associated with Yamato Takeru, including the Shiratori Mound, Kimiyazaki Fusotsuka, and Hokanzuka, all tied to legends of the warrior.
* [[Kannonji (Takatsuka, Suzuka)|Kannonji Temple]] * Suzuka Flower Park
== External Links == * [https://www.kasado-jinja.com/ Kasado Shrine Official Website]
